title: Polystichum craspedosorum
taxonomy: Polystichum craspedosorum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a member of the genus Polystichum, which comprises about 260 species of evergreen, perennial ferns. The Dryopteridaceae family is a group of ferns commonly known as wood ferns or shield ferns. They are native to subtropical and temperate regions around the world. The classification of Polystichum craspedosorum is further supported by its binomial nomenclature, which is a two-part scientific name consisting of the genus and species names.

intro: Polystichum craspedosorum, also known as the Chinese fern or Lanzhou fern, is a species of fern native to China and other parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Polystichum craspedosorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often made into herbal teas, decoctions, or infusions, as well as being dried and used in capsule or tablet form for its medicinal benefits.
